focus: Senior golfer distance struggles, losing distance over time, 3-wood abandonment, equipment vs swing debate, driver fitting needs

PAIN.quote: What perplexes me is the very slow ball speed and short distance. I'm currently playing to a 16 handicap (genuinely), and those driving stats seem very poor. Admittedly, my strength is approach shots and putting, but I hit my mid-irons and scoring irons very well. Driving seems to be the weakness
PAIN.context: 16 handicap perplexed by poor driving stats

PAIN.quote: I used to hit that thing further, now I can't. What Trackman 'said' aligned with my own experience on the course, over the past few months
Poor workman, or poor tools? Or both? A lesson, or a new driver? Or both? By the way I just turned 50, is becoming geriatric the reason for the short tee shot distance?
PAIN.context: 50 year old questioning if age is the cause

PAIN.quote: He was always faaaarrr longer than me, these days he's practically playing the next hole before I catch up!
As a senior golfer, I am distance handicapped. After my tee shot I am out of iron range most of the time. For me, it's either hit an iron and play for an up and down or reach for a hybrid / 5w
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club
PAIN.context: Senior always out of iron range

PAIN.quote: I've given up on a 3w because I can't hit it any further than a 5w
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

PAIN.quote: I long wistfully for the days when I could carry that fairway bunker at 250 yards on that par 5 many years ago. A mishit and I was in the bunker. That was fun. Now? Most drives are around 200 yards
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

PAIN.quote: I've lost a bit of distance with the driver over the past year as well. I'm chalking it up to letting my physical fitness level slip and not making good contact with the center of the face

HOPE.quote: You could just be the mystical 'gained 20 yards from a proper fitting' guy
I've found the best hybrids for me is the Ping HL - I carry a 5h and 3h. In the woods department, I landed on the Callaway 5w AI Smoke Max D
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

HOPE.quote: On occasion I find that the 3h can give me more distance than the 5w just because I can get more swing speed with the lighter and shorter club
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

HOPE.quote: In 2012, I switched to 4W + 7W and have played that ever since. I started having trouble with a 3W about 2010
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

HOPE.quote: If you can't reach a green in regulation regularly, it's time to move up a box. This game's supposed to be fun
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

HOPE.quote: I'm 79 and just had a year out being treated (successfully) for prostate cancer and my 'bombproof' knees are now not. What did I do? I read Tom Wishon's 'The Search for the Perfect Golf Club'
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Is it possible you haven't hit your driver as far as you may have thought? Comparing your estimated distances from course play to actual TrackMan stats can alone be quite different. And with many if not most, quite disappointing
A new driver probably isn't gonna make a significant difference in distance. Let's see your driver swing video yes?
ROOT_CAUSE.context: New driver won't fix swing issues

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Everyone always looks at equipment and sometimes lessons which are both worthy factors in distance loss
It's EXTREMELY fade biased, the P-TD. In my testing it was difficult to not push it 30 yards right. It sits wide open and the default lie angle is fairly flat

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I play from the senior tees
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I will hit it the odd time though if I really have to. Even though I carry a 3wd, it usually just holds my towel
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: For max distance, it's a 4W. Personally, I started having trouble with a 3W about 2010
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: A lot of times, I will lay up to pw or 9i distance
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Some of it is your swing, but I think your driver just isn't right for you
I checked out a couple of different woods and landed on the Callaway 5w AI Smoke Max D
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: I have a 8 degree lofted up to 10 in draw setting and it is perfect for my power fade without ever losing it too far right. With my swing it's still hard to turn over, loving this setup as I don't ever want to miss left
You definitely should. I found the Paradym TD to be very neutral biased. The Rogue ST TD was more fade biased

VILLAIN.quote: Carry: 182 yards. Total: 212 yards. Ball speed: 125 mph. What perplexes me is the very slow ball speed and short distance
I've noticed the diminished distance off the tee for the past two years
Be sure to take fitness and the swing itself into account when asking yourself why you're losing distance
After my accident in 2020 at 62 YOA I played the Mid tees at like 6400 at sea level. I was still long for my age then. After my accident when I came back I moved up to the Senior tees
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club

sources_covered: GolfWRX - Hey Seniors Max Distance Club
high_value_themes_identified: 50yo questioning if age is cause of distance loss
Senior always out of iron range
